terms.title: Hard Constraint
terms.content: A rule that must be satisfied. Violations make the schedule infeasible.

terms.title: Soft Constraint
terms.content: A preference rule. Violations are allowed but reduce objective score via penalties.

terms.title: Static Constraint
terms.content: A global rule that applies system-wide, not tied to a specific worker pair.

terms.title: Dynamic Constraint
terms.content: A pair-specific rule, usually involving two workers (for example exclusion or pairing).

terms.title: Task Option (OR)
terms.content: Alternative staffing patterns for a task. Solver chooses one option per task.

terms.title: Requirement (AND)
terms.content: All requirements inside the chosen option must be met simultaneously.

terms.title: Time Window
terms.content: Start and end boundaries that define when a worker can work or when a shift occurs.

terms.title: Objective Score
terms.content: The optimization score produced by the solver, combining rewards and penalties.

terms.title: Efficiency
terms.content: Displayed as objective_value/theoretical_max_score as a percentage for orientation.

terms.title: Global Violation Mapping
terms.content: Process that maps global penalty violations onto assignment cards for visual explainability.

terms.title: Round-Trip State
terms.content: An export that can be re-imported to restore workers, shifts, and constraints.

terms.title: Import Validation
terms.content: Pre-checks that enforce required sheets/columns and normalize known format variants.

terms.title: Skill Level Threshold
terms.content: Minimum level required for a worker to satisfy a requirement for a skill.
